title: Upgrade Parent Dashboard Live Chat with Real-Time Messaging Features

requirements.summary: Implement React Query polling loop for real-time message delivery in ParentDashboard and StudentMessagesPage
acceptanceCriteria: New messages sent by one party appear on the other party's screen within 2 seconds without any manual page refresh.
Polling starts when the chat section is mounted and stops when it unmounts.

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks for chat operations: useGetMessages (with 1-second refetchInterval), useSendMessage mutation, and useMarkMessagesRead mutation. The useGetMessages hook must call the backend getMessages function and enable polling when the component is mounted. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing to ensure proper caller context handling.

Replace the existing localStorage-based chat implementation with the new useGetMessages hook configured with a 1-second polling interval. The hook should be enabled only when the chat panel is visible. Remove all localStorage chat logic and wire in useSendMessage and useMarkMessagesRead mutations.

Replace the existing localStorage-based chat implementation with the new useGetMessages hook configured with a 1-second polling interval. Remove all localStorage chat logic and wire in useSendMessage and useMarkMessagesRead mutations.

requirements.summary: Add online/offline presence tracking with backend state and frontend display
acceptanceCriteria: A green indicator and 'Online' label appear when the other party has been active within 60 seconds.
A grey indicator and 'Last seen HH:MM' appear otherwise.
Presence updates are sent to the backend every 30 seconds while the chat is open.

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks: useUpdatePresence mutation (calls backend updatePresence) and useGetPresence query (calls backend getPresence with a 5-second refetchInterval). Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing to ensure proper authentication handling.

Add presence tracking: call useUpdatePresence every 30 seconds using a useEffect with setInterval while the chat is open. Fetch the linked student's presence using useGetPresence and display a green dot with 'Online' if lastSeen is within 60 seconds, or a grey dot with 'Last seen HH:MM' otherwise in the chat header.

Add presence tracking: call useUpdatePresence every 30 seconds using a useEffect with setInterval while the chat is open. Fetch the parent's presence using useGetPresence and display a green dot with 'Online' if lastSeen is within 60 seconds, or a grey dot with 'Last seen HH:MM' otherwise in the chat header.

requirements.summary: Add typing indicator with backend state and frontend display
acceptanceCriteria: Typing indicator appears within 1 second of the remote party starting to type.
Typing indicator disappears within 4 seconds after the remote party stops typing.
The animated ellipsis is visible and styled to match the chat bubble design.

file_operations.description: Add React Query hooks: useSetTyping mutation (calls backend setTyping) and useGetTypingStatus query (calls backend getTypingStatus with a 1-second refetchInterval). Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing to ensure proper caller context.

Add typing indicator logic: on input field onChange, call useSetTyping(true) and set a 3-second debounced timeout to call useSetTyping(false). Poll the student's typing status using useGetTypingStatus and display 'Student is typing...' with an animated ellipsis (three dots pulsing with CSS animation) below the message list when isTyping is true.

Add typing indicator logic: on input field onChange, call useSetTyping(true) and set a 3-second debounced timeout to call useSetTyping(false). Poll the parent's typing status using useGetTypingStatus and display 'Parent is typing...' with an animated ellipsis (three dots pulsing with CSS animation) below the message list when isTyping is true.

requirements.summary: Display formatted timestamps and date separators with auto-scroll
acceptanceCriteria: Each message displays a timestamp formatted as HH:MM AM/PM.
Date separators appear between message groups of different calendar days.
The message container scrolls to the bottom automatically on new messages and on initial load.

file_operations.description: Add timestamp formatting: convert each message's backend Time (bigint nanoseconds) to a JavaScript Date, then format as 'HH:MM AM/PM' and display beneath each chat bubble. Insert centered date separator components (e.g., 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'Dec 12') between message groups of different calendar days. Add a useEffect with a ref to scroll the message container to the bottom whenever the messages array changes or on initial load.

Add timestamp formatting: convert each message's backend Time (bigint nanoseconds) to a JavaScript Date, then format as 'HH:MM AM/PM' and display beneath each chat bubble. Insert centered date separator components (e.g., 'Today', 'Yesterday', 'Dec 12') between message groups of different calendar days. Add a useEffect with a ref to scroll the message container to the bottom whenever the messages array changes or on initial load.

requirements.summary: Redesign chat UI with modern bubbles, avatars, and mobile-responsive layout
acceptanceCriteria: Outgoing bubbles are right-aligned with the app's primary colour.
Incoming bubbles are left-aligned with a contrasting neutral colour.
The chat input bar is always visible at the bottom of the chat pane on all screen sizes.
The UI renders correctly on 375px width (mobile) and 1280px width (desktop).

file_operations.description: Redesign the chat UI: outgoing parent messages styled as right-aligned bubbles with a primary background color (from Tailwind config), incoming student messages as left-aligned bubbles with a neutral background. Add sender avatar initials in a circular badge beside each bubble. Ensure the message list uses flex-1 or min-h-0 to take available vertical space, and the input bar is pinned to the bottom using a fixed or sticky layout. Test responsiveness at 375px and 1280px widths.

Redesign the chat UI: outgoing student messages styled as right-aligned bubbles with a primary background color (from Tailwind config), incoming parent messages as left-aligned bubbles with a neutral background. Add sender avatar initials in a circular badge beside each bubble. Ensure the message list uses flex-1 or min-h-0 to take available vertical space, and the input bar is pinned to the bottom using a fixed or sticky layout. Test responsiveness at 375px and 1280px widths.

requirements.summary: Show in-app toast and browser push notifications for new messages
acceptanceCriteria: A toast notification appears when a new message arrives and the chat panel is not active.
A browser push notification fires when the document.visibilityState is 'hidden' and the user has granted notification permission.
Clicking the notification navigates the user to the chat section.

file_operations.description: Add notification logic: in the useGetMessages query's onSuccess callback, compare new messages with the previous messages array. If a new incoming message is detected and the chat panel is not in focus or visible, trigger a toast notification using the existing toast provider. If document.visibilityState is 'hidden' and Notification.permission is 'granted', send a browser push notification (via the existing service worker registration) with the sender's name and message preview. Ensure clicking the notification navigates to the chat section.

Add notification logic: in the useGetMessages query's onSuccess callback, compare new messages with the previous messages array. If a new incoming message is detected and the page is not in focus or visible, trigger a toast notification using the existing toast provider. If document.visibilityState is 'hidden' and Notification.permission is 'granted', send a browser push notification (via the existing service worker registration) with the sender's name and message preview. Ensure clicking the notification navigates to the chat section.

requirements.summary: Add network error handling with retry logic and user feedback
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Connection lost' banner appears when a backend call fails.
Failed message sends are retried up to 3 times automatically.
After 3 failures, a visible error state with a 'Retry' button is shown.
The banner dismisses automatically when connectivity is restored and a request succeeds.

file_operations.description: Configure React Query mutations (useSendMessage) with retry: 3 and exponential backoff (retryDelay: attemptIndex => Math.min(1000 * 2 ** attemptIndex, 30000)). Add onError handlers to capture network failures.

Add network error handling: display a non-blocking banner ('Connection lost – retrying…') when useSendMessage or useGetMessages returns an error due to network issues. After 3 failed retries, show a persistent error state with a 'Retry' button that manually triggers the mutation again. Dismiss the banner automatically when a subsequent request succeeds.

Add network error handling: display a non-blocking banner ('Connection lost – retrying…') when useSendMessage or useGetMessages returns an error due to network issues. After 3 failed retries, show a persistent error state with a 'Retry' button that manually triggers the mutation again. Dismiss the banner automatically when a subsequent request succeeds.

requirements.summary: Update unread message badge in Layout to reflect backend unread count
acceptanceCriteria: The unread badge in the sidebar/nav reflects the backend unread count.
The badge clears when the user opens and views the chat.

file_operations.description: Replace the current localStorage-based unread message count logic with a call to useGetMessages (from useQueries.ts). Extract the unread count from the returned messages array (filter messages where isRead is false and senderId is not the current user). Display this count in the unread badge next to the Messages navigation link. The badge should automatically clear when the user navigates to the messages page and markMessagesRead is called. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing to ensure proper caller context.
